sequence.title: Step by Step
steps: Drag blocks into the workspace one at a time.
Blockly connects them in a chain — the top block runs first.
Press Run and watch each block light up in the order it runs.

turn.title: Turning
steps: Drag a 'turn left' or 'turn right' block below your last block.
Turning doesn't move the robot — it only changes which way it's facing.
Add a 'move forward' block right after it to actually travel in the new direction.

loop.title: Repeat Blocks
steps: Drag a 'repeat' block into your workspace.
Type how many times it should repeat — like 3, or 10.
Drag the block you want to repeat (like 'move forward') INSIDE the repeat block.
One repeat block now does the work of many separate blocks — try putting a turn inside it too!

conditional.title: Making Choices
steps: Drag an 'if' block into your workspace.
Drop 'path ahead clear?' into the if block's checking slot.
Put 'move forward' inside the if — it only runs when the path is really clear.
Click the if block's gear icon to add an 'else' — that's what happens when the path is NOT clear, like turning instead.

variable.title: Remembering Numbers
steps: Open the Variables drawer and make a new variable, like "steps".
Drag a 'set steps to' block in and type a number.
Now drag the "steps" variable itself into a repeat block's number slot, instead of typing a number.
Change the variable's value once, and everywhere you used it updates automatically.

obstacleAvoidance.title: Watching for Walls
steps: Before moving into a new cell, check 'path ahead clear?' with an if block.
If it's clear, 'move forward' like normal.
If it's not clear, turn instead of crashing — try putting 'turn right' in the else.
Combine this with a repeat block to keep checking and moving all the way to the goal.

nudge.idle: Not sure where to start? Drag a block into the workspace and press Run — you can always try again!
nudge.hintIntro: I've got a hint for this level — check the hints panel below. Hints cost a little XP, like buying a clue.
nudge.genericHint: Try changing the order of your blocks, or how many times a loop repeats.
nudge.explainOffer: This level is all about {{concept}}. Want me to walk you through it, step by step?

solution.title: How to Solve This Level
solution.intro: Here's one way to solve it, step by step — I'll build it in your workspace so you can see exactly how the pieces fit together.
solution.buildConfirm: This will replace whatever's in your workspace right now — build the solution anyway?
solution.actionSetVariable: Set the variable "{{name}}" to {{value}} — like writing a number on a sticky note to use later.
solution.repeatVariable: Repeat as many times as "{{name}}" says:
solution.ifCondition: Check: is the path ahead clear?
